allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking strengthens cybersecurity-thinking-models by revealing cross-layer interdependencies and feedback paths.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking is the discipline for seeing interrelationships rather than things.

allies.rationale_text: Critical-thinking strengthens cybersecurity-thinking-models by forcing assumption checks and evidence review inside adversarial analysis.
allies.source_quote: Critical thinking, which involves evaluating evidence and noticing bias, is the how of learning.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with confirmation-bias when defenders process threat evidence toward the conclusion they already prefer.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when motivated reasoning causes security teams to process threat evidence toward a desired conclusion rather than genuine evaluation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with incentives when security measurement rewards appearing secure instead of being secure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with incentives (incentives) when stakeholder incentives are not aligned with the security outcome being measured, so defenders optimize for appearing secure rather than being secure.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with brainstorming when divergent scenario generation is collapsed before adversarial hypotheses are pressure-tested.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with brainstorming (brainstorming) when divergent scenario generation is prematurely collapsed by convergent evaluation, suppressing the adversarial hypotheses most needed for robust threat modeling.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
